##NAMES=Greenlizardite
##RRUFFID=R250059
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=(NH_4_)Na(UO_2_)_2_(SO_4_)_2_(OH)_2_·4H_2_O
##LOCALITY=Green Lizard mine, Red Canyon, San Juan County, Utah, USA
##OWNER=Natural History Museum of Los Angeles County
##SOURCE=Tony Kampf
##DESCRIPTION=Light green-yellow blades associated with ammoniozippeite, boussingaultite and dickite. This is the type sample described in Kampf et al (2018)
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral is confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R250059

##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Single crystal, powder profile is calculated
